behavior_summary: FLOOR::gotHit is the U8P_FLOOR::gotHit event handler for a floor object. When invoked it records or updates an information entry (info code 0x20C) on the floor object using the first argument, then calls a 'process exclude' operation to prevent further/reentrant handling related to that info change.

The routine also copies the second argument into a short local ([BP-02h]) and compares it to 1; in the provided excerpt that comparison does not alter control flow (both outcomes return), so its observable effect is unclear from this snippet.
Overall the handler appears to log a hit-related value on the floor and mark the object/process as excluded from further immediate processing.
notable_control_flow: Linear sequence: set info (0x20C) -> process exclude -> store second parameter to local -> compare local to 1 -> return.
The comparison of [BP-02h] against 1 is present but does not change return behavior in the provided excerpt, so it currently appears to be a non-branching conditional or a vestigial check.
open_questions: What semantic meaning does info code 0x20C have (e.g., last hitter id, hit type, timestamp)?
What side-effects or broader engine behavior does 'process exclude' trigger for the floor object (temporary ignore, permanent disable, AI exclusion)?
What is the intended effect of the [BP-02h] == 1 comparison in contexts not present in the excerpt — does other code rely on this local being set or checked differently?
Are there additional handlers or scripts that respond to info 0x20C being set on the floor object?

main_function.name: U8P_FLOOR::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle a hit event on a floor object by storing hit-related data (info id 0x20C) associated with the floor and excluding the process/object from further immediate handling; also copy a small parameter into a local and compare it to 1 (effect unclear).
entry_conditions: Invoked on Event 6 (U8P_FLOOR::gotHit) for the floor object — triggered when the object receives a hit event from an actor or effect.

function_map.name: U8P_FLOOR::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for U8P_FLOOR::gotHit on the floor object; records info and excludes the process.

function_map.name: U8P_FLOOR::set info
function_map.role: Engine call to store/update an info key (used here with id 0x20C) on a target object.

function_map.name: U8P_FLOOR::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine call that marks the referenced process/object to be excluded from further processing (prevents reentrant or duplicate handling).

external_calls.purpose: Record/update the info entry id 0x20C on the floor object using the first argument as the stored value or identifier.
Exclude or mark the process/object to avoid further immediate processing after the info key is set (likely a guard against reentrancy/duplicate effects).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: FLOOR::gotHit is the U8P_FLOOR::gotHit event handler for a floor object. When invoked it records or updates an information entry (info code 0x20C) on the floor object using the first argument, then calls a 'process exclude' operation to prevent further/reentrant handling related to that info change.
